title: LeaseId-fencing invariant has no anchor comment in clearOwnerLeaseFile and clearOwnerLeaseFileIfOwner
claim: The fence invariant (always re-validate the lease immediately before mutation) is explicitly anchored with WHY comments in acquireOwnerLeaseFile (lines 525-528, 530-534) and refreshOwnerLeaseFile (lines 564-567), but has NO equivalent anchor in the two clear functions
subIssue: clearOwnerLeaseFile second-read at line 616 checks ownerPid only (NOT leaseId), creating asymmetry with refreshOwnerLeaseFile second-read at line 576 which checks both fields; no comment explains the asymmetry
counterevidenceSought: Asymmetric protection is logically sufficient because the only path for leaseId change requires acquireOwnerLeaseFile which always changes ownerPid first — but this property is undocumented
alternativeExplanation: Implementer understood the asymmetric protection but simply did not write the same anchor in cleanup paths because cleanup is called only on graceful shutdown
downgradeTrigger: If implementer documents both invariant AND asymmetric-protection reasoning AND demonstrates via test that the asymmetry is exercised safely, downgrades to P3 informational
recommendation: Add a one-paragraph anchor comment above clearOwnerLeaseFile (line 611) explaining why the function re-reads the lease immediately before unlink AND why the second reread is ownerPid-only. Same for clearOwnerLeaseFileIfOwner (line 628) which has no leaseId anchor at all.
adjacentFinding: Bounded by active P1-001 (algorithmic generation-check gap); P2-003 is the documentation/maintainability dimension of the same underlying area

notes: iter 7 does not re-test spec_code; iter 6 results stand
iter 7 does not re-test checklist; iter 6 results stand

recommendIter8: Adversarial re-verification of P1-001: construct Scenarios A (refresh late-arrival), B (clear cross-process), C (heartbeat stale-restart) and adjudicate final severity
recommendIter9: P2-001 plan.md phase-label cleanup OR new test coverage for refresh/clear TOCTOU paths
recommendIter10: Synthesis: final verdict, cumulative finding list, dimension coverage map, release-readiness call

resolution: none — all writes within allowed paths
